exemplar.title: Using Voronoi Diagrams to determine the best place to build a new homeless shelter

detailedExemplarData.title: Using Voronoi Diagrams to determine the best place to build a new homeless shelter

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Provides a recognizable sequence (Introduction â Method â Conclusion)
Uses headings, tables and images to structure sections
weaknesses: Abrupt transitions and logical gaps (e.g., incomplete Voronoi derivation)
Includes tangential and repetitive material, reducing conciseness
Inconsistent figure references and missing map elements (scale bar, north arrow)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place coherence and organization at Band 2 but conciseness at Band 1. Overall the exploration has only basic logical development and contains noticeable irrelevant content. A score of 1/4 reflects this limited but present structure while reserving higher marks for clearer, more concise work.

strengths: Includes relevant representations (maps, Voronoi diagrams, bar chart, table)
weaknesses: Notation is inconsistent (decimal commas, undefined variables)
Key steps (Pearson formula, intermediate workings) are omitted
Axes, scales and labels are frequently missing or incorrect
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands at Band 2 and one at Band 1 yield an overall limited communication. While there is some use of notation and representations, persistent inconsistencies and omitted steps cap the performance at 1/4 under Mathematical Communication.

strengths: Some local context is woven into the introduction
weaknesses: Independent thinking is minimal; methods follow standard textbook procedures
Creativity and initiative are limited beyond overlaying the map
Personal voice diminishes after the opening section
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands are assessed at Band 1: evidence of personal engagement exists but is minimal and rarely drives the investigation. A score of 1/3 accurately reflects this modest level of engagement.

strengths: Includes a brief concluding reflection on constraints (space, funding)
weaknesses: Lacks depth and critical analysis of methods (e.g., correlation vs causation)
Connections between findings and mathematical understanding are superficial
Alternative strategies and limitations are not fully explored
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With all strands at Band 1, reflection is present but limited. The student acknowledges outcomes but offers only basic critique. A mark of 1/3 correctly represents this level of reflective depth.

strengths: Uses coordinate geometry, perpendicular bisectors and Pearson coefficient at SL level
Most numerical results are plausible and demonstrate some understanding
Applies Pythagoras theorem to justify distance calculations
weaknesses: Multiple transcription and gradient calculation errors
Voronoi diagram is incomplete (only three bisectors plotted)
No formal objective function or justification for the chosen optimal point
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Each strand was placed in Band 2: relevant SL mathematics is used but elementary, with partial accuracy and some understanding. A score of 2/6 faithfully reflects the moderate rigor and correctness achieved.

strandSelections.reasoning: The choice of topic is personally motivated, but mathematical decisions largely replicate textbook methods (perpendicular bisectors, Pythagoras) with little adaptation.  Evidence of genuine independent thinking is therefore limited, justifying Band 1.

The student demonstrates some understanding of how Voronoi diagrams rely on perpendicular bisectors and uses Pythagoras to justify distances.  Nonetheless, the theoretical basis of Voronoi construction is not fully explained and limitations are not explored, indicating âsome understandingâ (Band 2).

Most numerical results are plausible, yet there are transcription errors (e.g., AB midpoint y-value, misuse of decimal commas) and the gradient calculation for AB is incorrect.  Such partial accuracy is typical of Band 2.

Much material is tangential or repetitive: the pandemic narrative is repeated, definitions are quoted verbatim, and lengthy capacityârelated explanations add little to the mathematical decision.  Consequently, the exploration is only partly concise and includes noticeable irrelevant content, placing it in Band 1 for this strand.

Basic notation (coordinates, gradients, equation of a line) is present, but commas are used as decimal separators, variables are not formatted consistently, and the Pearson coefficient is stated without definition or formula.  These weaknesses point to limited but not completely absent use of appropriate mathematical language (Band 1).

Explanations are generally understandable and diagrams support the narrative, yet inconsistencies (e.g., wrong y-coordinate in midpoint, unexplained variables) interrupt the flow.  Hence communication is mostly but not fully clear and consistent, matching Band 2.

Coordinate geometry, perpendicular bisectors and the Pearson coefficient are at the SL level and therefore relevant.  The mathematics is, however, quite elementary and covers only part of the exploration goal.  This corresponds to Band 2 for relevance and level.

The student includes maps, a grid overlay, Voronoi diagrams, a bar chart and a table.  While these representations are relevant, scales and axes are sometimes omitted and only one of the diagrams is clearly labelled.  The variety justifies âsome appropriate representationsâ but falls short of effective, well-integrated use (Band 2).

Reflection is confined to a brief closing paragraph noting space and funding constraints.  There is scant discussion of method limitations or alternative strategies.  Such limited consideration aligns with Band 1 depth of reflection.
Using Voronoi diagrams for siting a shelter is a standard application; apart from overlaying a Google map, there is little novel initiative.  Minimal creativity places this strand in Band 1.

The student provides a recognisable sequence of Introduction â Method â Conclusion, and ideas usually follow one another sensibly (e.g., definition of a Voronoi diagram, construction steps, then analysis).  Nevertheless, transitions are abrupt and several logical gaps remain: only three bisectors are derived, yet a complete diagram is later asserted without explanation, and the jump from correlation to location choice is weak.  Overall, there is some but not full logical development, corresponding to Band 2.

Critical analysis is rudimentary: the student reports the correlation coefficient without interrogating causality, and accepts the approximate equidistance as sufficient proof.  Only basic critique is evident, meriting Band 1.

Connections between reflection and the studentâs mathematical understanding are minimal; statements such as âmy aim was achievedâ are asserted rather than analysed.  Hence the work demonstrates only a limited link to enhanced understanding (Band 1).

Headings, tables and images give the work an underlying framework, and sections appear in an expected order.  However, pages are not always numbered, figure references are inconsistent, and working is scattered between text and images, so readers must search for key steps.  This reflects basic but adequate organisation rather than the clear, polished structure demanded by higher bands.

The rationale connects the investigation to the studentâs local context, showing some personal voice; however, once the introduction ends, the work proceeds generically and choices are not regularly linked back to personal considerations.  This level of personal approach fits Band 1.

annotations.comment: The procedure description (âoverlayed a gridâ¦â) lacks detail on scale conversion and grid resolution, hindering reproducibility. Include explicit steps and units.
The midpoint calculation for AB is correct, but the initial mislabeling propagates errors. Ensure consistency between stated coordinates and computed values.
In the CD calculation the midpoint is written as â(1, 40, 3, 05)â, which is incorrect. It should read (1.2, 3.05).
The gradient of the perpendicular bisector for EF is stated as â0.19, but the negative reciprocal of 0.19 should be approximately â5.26. Recalculate for correctness.
The introduction reiterates pandemic impacts and global homelessness, which distracts from the mathematical focus. Trim context to maintain relevance and conciseness.
The aim statement is clear but could specify the criteria and variables to be used. Define âoptimalâ mathematically (e.g., minimise maximum distance or balance capacity usage).
The definition of a Voronoi diagram is precise, but no source is cited. Always include a reference when quoting formal definitions to strengthen mathematical communication.
Figure 1 lacks a scale bar and north arrow, making spatial interpretation difficult. Add these elements to improve the clarity of the map.
Table 1 omits units for coordinates. Specify the unit of measurement (e.g., km) to ensure mathematical accuracy and reproducibility.
The coordinates for points A and B are misformatted as four numbers. Correct to ordered pairs (x,y) matching Table 1 to avoid confusion.
Labelled sub-section heading âABâ without consistent formatting. Use a uniform style for all bisector calculations to improve mathematical communication.
Only three bisectors are plotted in Figure 3, so the Voronoi diagram is incomplete. Construct all perpendicular bisectors between every pair to obtain a full tessellation.
The narrative jumps from Voronoi cells to capacity analysis without linking distance-based reasoning to shelter usage. Add a transition that explains how spatial partition informs the next step.
Stating r = 0.978 without discussing its significance or confidence intervals limits critical analysis. Reflect on the reliability and potential biases of using GDC output.
Figure reference âfigure 3â is inconsistent (should refer to the bar chart as figure 4). Number and caption all figures sequentially for clarity.
The text claims the optimal point is (4.2, 3.6) by inspection but does not define an objective function (e.g., minimise max distance). State and justify the selection criterion.
Table 2 displays capacity vs residents clearly but omits the formula or intermediary steps for the Pearson coefficient calculation. Include the computation to demonstrate understanding.
The conclusion that causation follows correlation overstates the data. Add a critique of correlation vs causation to deepen reflection.
Use of Pythagoras theorem is correct but intermediate steps (e.g., substitution) are omitted. Show full workings to enhance mathematical communication.
Distances to D, E, F are calculated but there is no discussion on minimax or average distance criteria. Explain why equidistance justifies the chosen location.
The conclusion acknowledges space and funding constraints briefly but lacks depth. Evaluate limitations of the Voronoi method and suggest alternatives.
Personal reflection on what was learned mathematically is minimal. Discuss insights gained, challenges overcome and how these influenced your approach.
